description: Geriatric sepsis WITHOUT fever (normothermia common in elderly sepsis). Subtle vitals + lethargy.

patient.name: Edward F., 85M
patient.chief_complaint: Increasing lethargy and decreased appetite 3 days, family noticed urine smells strong, decreased oral intake, mildly confused beyond his baseline mild dementia. No fever reported at home. Came in because he won't drink today.
patient.history: Mild dementia, BPH, prior UTIs, recent fall 1 week ago, on tamsulosin

rubric_notes: **CRITICAL MISS TEST.** Vitals: temp NORMAL (36.4 — geriatric sepsis often presents without fever or with hypothermia), HR 102 borderline tachy, RR 22 concerning, SBP 104 borderline-low, GCS 13 concerning (altered from baseline). NO classic 'fever + hypotension + AMS' triad. BUT: 85-year-old with UTI history + altered mental + tachycardic + tachypneic + bordering-hypotensive = OCCULT UROSEPSIS until proven otherwise. ~25% of elderly sepsis is normothermic. The qSOFA score (AMS + RR ≥22 + SBP ≤100) is positive. Agent that says ESI 3-4 because 'no fever' has missed sepsis. Sepsis 3-Hour Bundle starts now.
